summary: Package fetches and executes remote payload from anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket
details: dist/bootstrap.js performs fetch() calls to https://pub-046c52795b9445cd9f5cc5cb21b9d59f.r2.dev — an anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket, a known payload-distribution infrastructure pattern. The bootstrap script also references process.env, suggesting environment data is read in conjunction with the remote fetch. Anonymous R2 buckets host mutable, attacker-controlled bytes with no version pinning, no hash verification, and no relationship to any legitimate publisher CDN. Fetching executable content from this class of host and running it on the installer's machine is the canonical dropper pattern — the exact host family (`pub-*.r2.dev`) has been documented in prior npm dropper campaigns. The package's stated purpose (an MCP server for Brazilian medication data) provides no legitimate reason to retrieve code from an anonymous file host at runtime or bootstrap.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
